channel.title: Dns configuration in zeit.co
channel.description: Hi, I would like to know if it is possible to redirect all traffic from my url ( **[mydomain.com](http://mydomain.com/)** ) to **[zeit.co](http://zeit.co/)** and from there redirect **[wp.mydomain.com](http://wp.mydomain.com/)** to my content server. That way I could edit the contents using the Wordpress dashboard, going to **[wp.myadomain.com](http://wp.myadomain.com/)** Tks

item.title: Dns configuration in zeit.co
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
By the way, you don’t need to change your DNS’s to use Zeit. You can add a CNAME record pointing to alias.zeit.co to any domain that you want to use with them.

Read full topic

item.title: Dns configuration in zeit.co
item.dc:creator: sonicares
item.description:
Also just found this

Hawk Host Community Forums

Able to change nameserver for subdomain?

Im hosting a full stack app via Zeit Now, and was wanting to have a subdomain on my domain parked on Hawk Host. However, Zeit requires me to edit the Nameservers on the subdomain, but I only find the ability to do this for the entire domain. Is there...

Looks like the method of using TXT record to direct the main domain to zeit and then using A records with your registrar to redirect your subdomain wordpress install to a regular host should work perfectly.

Read full topic

item.title: Dns configuration in zeit.co
item.dc:creator: sonicares
item.description:
I was also looking at this. There is a way to do it with Cnames on your registrar and not using zeit name servers. then you can redirect wp.mydomain.com from your registrar using A record to your wordpress install. I havent tried it though. Let me know if it works. I might use it myself.

ZEIT Documentation

Setup a Namecheap Domain with ZEIT Now - ZEIT Now Guides

Use your Namecheap registered domain for your ZEIT Now deployments.

Read full topic

item.title: Dns configuration in zeit.co
item.dc:creator: vscopise
item.description:
Hi,
I would like to know if it is possible to redirect all traffic from my url ( mydomain.com ) to zeit.co and from there redirect wp.mydomain.com to my content server.
That way I could edit the contents using the Wordpress dashboard, going to wp.myadomain.com
Tks

Read full topic
